nodes.label: 9. Why this stack (and not the alternatives)
nodes.norm_label: 9. why this stack (and not the alternatives)

nodes.label: Public Browser Key Rationale
nodes.rationale: The frontend runs in the browser, so the YouTube Data API key is public and should be restricted by HTTP referrer.

nodes.label: Stream Proxy Rationale
nodes.rationale: Some direct media URLs are bound to the API server IP, so browser playback should use the backend /stream proxy.

nodes.label: Base64 Cookie Secret Rationale
nodes.rationale: Base64 is preferred for hosted cookie secrets because it avoids multiline copy and paste problems.

nodes.label: Sidecar Decision
nodes.rationale: BotGuard changes frequently, so pulling an upstream sidecar image keeps token generation current and isolates JavaScript runtime crashes from FastAPI.

nodes.label: HTTP PO Token Provider Decision
nodes.rationale: An HTTP provider caches tokens across requests, while script mode spawns Node per call and is slower under load.

nodes.label: FastAPI Decision
nodes.rationale: yt-dlp is synchronous and blocking; FastAPI with a threadpool and semaphore caps concurrency without rewriting yt-dlp.

nodes.label: Static UI Separation Decision
nodes.rationale: The UI is fully static and CORS-enabled, so hosting it separately lets the backend move without breaking the public URL.
